steps.title: Identify the zeros of f(x)
steps.content: The four points with $y = 0$ are the zeros of $f(x)$. Reading from the graph: the roots are $\{-6, 7, 0, 4\}$.

steps.title: A factor must use only these roots
steps.content: If polynomial $q(x)$ divides $f(x)$, then every root of $q(x)$ must also be a root of $f(x)$. So we need to find which choice has roots only from the set $\{-6, 7, 0, 4\}$.

steps.title: Test choice D
steps.content: Backsolving — testing choice D ($x^2 - 7x$): Factor out $x$ to get $x(x - 7)$. The roots are $0$ and $7$, both of which are in our root set $\{-6, 7, 0, 4\}$. Therefore, $x(x-7)$ is a factor of $f(x)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
